name: chrome
description: Control the user's real, logged-in Chrome for tasks that depend on existing Chrome state: open tabs, logged-in sessions, cookies, and extensions. Includes a bundled Windows native host executable under extension-host/windows/x64/host.exe and drives Chrome through the local Chrome Control bridge. Prefer purpose-built connectors, APIs, or CLIs; use Chrome when the task needs the user's own browser state or the user explicitly asks for Chrome.

interface.shortDescription: Control your real Chrome with nexts-ai
interface.longDescription: nexts-ai uses your real, logged-in Chrome for tasks that need your existing browser state: open tabs, page content, and sites you're already signed into. It includes a bundled Windows native host executable, installs the Chrome Native Messaging manifest, and exposes a local Chrome Control bridge at 127.0.0.1:18765. It can navigate, view, click, type, fill, search, and take screenshots while working. You stay in control: nexts-ai confirms before sensitive actions, you can stop at any time, and you can manage or remove Chrome access in settings. Browser content may include sensitive information from logged-in sites.
